nouns.summary: The text states that there are specific counts of generations, listing three ranges of time and giving the number of generations in each range.
Abraham is presented as the starting point for the first listed sequence of generations, which runs from Abraham to David.
David is presented as an intermediate point in the genealogy, marking the end of the first generation range and the start of the next range.
The text uses “μετοικεσίας” to designate the period of relocation/exile (specifically connected to Babylon) and treats it as a boundary between generation ranges.
Babylon is named as the place associated with the relocation/exile, and the generation sequence is described in relation to the period “from the relocation of Babylon to Christ.”
Christ is presented as the final endpoint of the genealogy, completing the last listed generation range “from Babylon’s relocation to Christ.”}]}
